ops.title: Anchors: expose from cc-context (public pkg + ccx anchor CLI), swap cc-present to the dependency
ops.description: User redirect during the draft/triage build: cc-present must not carry a Go port of the ccx anchor scheme. Instead: (A) cc-context promotes `internal/anchor` to a public `anchor` package (API unchanged), updates internal imports, and adds a minimal `ccx anchor hash|resolve` CLI verb group; ship path-scoped. (B) cc-present deletes `internal/anchor/anchor.go` + port test, imports `github.com/yasyf/cc-context/anchor` (pseudo-version pin on the pushed commit is fine), adapts `internal/daemon/rest.go` to the upstream API (`FromBytes(...)` → `*File`, `File.Resolve(ref) (Range, *Move, error)`, `Parse` returning `(Ref, ok, err)` — ok=false is a 400 at the REST edge), keeps `internal/anchor/testdata/anchors.json` + a conformance test running the IMPORTED package over the local corpus (cross-repo drift alarm), and updates the contract.md Line anchors wording (daemon uses ccx's implementation; SPA + iOS carry conformant ports). Web/iOS anchor ports stay — client-side hashing/resolve is architecturally required.

ops.text: cc-context exposes a public anchor package with unchanged API and green gates; ccx anchor hash|resolve CLI verbs exist
cc-present builds with the cc-context dependency and no local anchor.go; conformance test runs the imported package over internal/anchor/testdata/anchors.json
web + iOS anchor conformance suites still pass unchanged against the same corpus
